channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: AI BUBBLE IS POPPING — 67 replies — 268.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI BUBBLE IS POPPING

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-03-07 01:41 UTC

OP

It’s finally happening. THIS IS WHY NVDA DUMPED AFTER EARNINGS

Early replies (first 3)

61946639
two more weeks

61946643
>>61946600
@grok is this true?

61946647
priced out and off kirk af

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61947524
>>61947386
What part of pro se do you not understand. This is me representing my self. I’m an IT guy not an attorney

61947539
>>61946691

61947547
>>61947401
Here’s a lesser-known trick many gastroenterologists mention that can dramatically reduce wiping.

The “wait and final push” method

After the main bowel movement, don’t stand up immediately.

Stay seated for about 15–30 seconds.

Relax your abdomen and pelvic floor.

Do one gentle final push (not straining).

Why it works:
The rectum sometimes holds a small amount of stool after the main movement. Waiting lets the Rectum finish moving the remaining stool down so it comes out in one go instead of later smearing when you wipe.

61947553
>>61946600
Everyone and all the financial channels say it's a bubble. So it must be, right?

61947556
>>61947436
cope
https://x.com/AndreasSteno/status/2029324762913104352

61947565
>>61947281
>pro se
you're going to get steamrolled by a real lawyer

61947583
>>61947556
>replies is just people talking to each other with slop copypasted from ChatGPT

LMAO

61947597
>>61946677
you fail to see the usefulness of this. I call it epistemic land mining and are escalating it to ontological terraforming

61947607
There is no AI bubble.
Not only is this stuff already actively being used. Before a bubble, not everyone and their mother is saying that a bubble might be coming. Instead, every normie thinks they’re about to get rich by investing

61947617
>>61947583
yeah, that's the mundane surface level of use of agentic set ups, trying to fake "consensus" so line go up. Boring

61947621
>>61947607
You are right but also wrong, its not a bubble, its a fucking scam.
We have billions and billions into AI and fuck all to show for it except videos of Oscer the Grouch fucking Taylor swift.
AI has largely been rejected by the working population, no one fucking wants it.
MS fucking flag ship OS is being openly rejected because of Co pilot
The hippy art community is shitting on it.
Massive portions of gaming are rejecting anything that uses AI
Vibe coding is a fucking joke.
The only place its been even remotely useful has been data analytics.

Other then that its literally just been nothing but a carnival ghimmic that costs fuckign BILLION.
and here is the best part, everyone knows this, companies are basically betting that it going to pay off and it will be some revolutionary tech but no one has produced anything that actually indicates thats gonna happen for a reasonable cost.

61947628
>>61947621
>data analytics
oh you poor fool

61947631
>>61947621
> companies are basically betting that it going to pay off and it will be some revolutionary tech
It already is that today you retard. Even back at chatGPT day 1, it put stack overflow in the dirt and has only gotten better since

61947633
>>61947621
Reddit is not real life.
People already use it every day for all kinds of things - cooking, translating, asking for cost estimates, medical and legal questions, and so on and so on.

61947644
>>61947631
>It already is that today you retard.
Its really fucking not.

Vibe coding is fucking awful it constantly will make syntax errors or just give you commands that tare out right fucking wrong. Its great to get a rough idea but its not replacing anyone soon.

Its a glorified search engine.
>>61947633
Yeah, so its basically a glorified google search. wow totally worth billions and billions of USD.

Anone whos in their 30s will use this and realize it basically gives you the same results as what google would give you 15 or so years ago before the internet became flooded with bullshit slop.
>Cooking
Yeah guess what 15 years ago i could look up how to make a simple turkey roast and find like 5 websites that were just giving me the recipe

Now? I gotta dig through 10 different sights and read a small novel about how this turkey recipe helped the author get over the fact their step father raped them.
or
Use chat GPT which jsut gives me the fucking results google would 15 years ago.

Current AI is a joke, i have used it professionally and its EH at best.

61947646
>>61947621
> scam
The same way the steam engine was a scam I suppose

61947647
>>61947621
>AI has largely been rejected by the working population, no one fucking wants it
Unironically because prompting is too hard for the zombie population. AI art made it possible for everyone to make their own drawings but 99% of the population is not even creative enough to write a prompt describing a drawing. All of them will cope and say that AI art isn't artistic enough but the real problem is the exact opposite, the braindead normalfags are not creative enough to even know what the fuck to do with an art producing machine. They need to be told what to want before they start wanting it

61947656
>>61947644
>a glorified google search
RAG is to put it mildly the end of truth

61947664
>>61947647
I mean yes a tool is only as good as the tool user, and unfortunately most people are fucking stupid.

Right now the most use AI is getting is being a therapist to people who just want to talk to someone.
And google 2.0. thats it thats what the majority of the users are using it for.
Thats not multi billion dollar value.

61947679
>>61947644
you're hallucinating more than AI does in your wildest fantasies

61947681
>>61947647
>the braindead normalfags are not creative enough
true, its black pilling how the extend of normal faggot creativity is copying hollywood characters and narratives, cyberpunk and cats engaging in anthropomorphized tomfoolery. The only positive thing is, that it losens the grab of the original propaganda even more; an normal faggot that prompts its own version of matrix, John wick and co is going to spend less time on the propaganda

61947702
>>61947644
pretty sure they shitted up actual search engines to encourage AI adoption

61947709
>>61947702
I dont think they are that smart, i think its a case of the internet is so fucking cluttered with junk and garbage that modern search engines just blow.

61947720
>>61946677
@grok summarize this tweet

61947723
>>61947647
>make their own drawings
>art producing machine
you aren't creating art. you're commanding a machine to generate an image in the style on which it was trained. normalfags understand this. fat autistic retards confuse it with creativity and art, in the same sense that they confuse chicken nuggies or ramen noodles with real food or anime OSTs with real music.

61947733
>>61946639
>t. zoomer with zero attention span
>everything must happen in two weeks for them to register

61947737
>>61947621
Its the existing economy that's a scam, we've far overvalued everything from fiat to crypto to precious metals off the back of needing to continually grow revenues to keep the economy from crashing. AI's purpose is not to scam, but to replace the entire basis of the economy with automation. Its not meant to have a return on investment, its meant to usher in an age where the rich no longer need to invest because they literally own all the labor and the labor is a device with no rights.

We've had burger flipping robots since the 60s, LLMs are just thinky-bits enough to make them viable as replacements for 90% of labor, both at the blue collar and the white collar ends. Yes, you'll always need some people to do actual work, but it was always a tiny minority of every company doing actual work.

61947740
>>61946677
Asking grok to verify the truth of something is different than asking for an explanation you fucking retard.

61947782
>>61947740
You do understand how rag works? Grok has no epistemic conscious, it has no truth or knowledge, it just aggregates data. I am currently running experiments on data poisoning and your lot is the reason I am escalating, its likely possible to destroy the current ontology of the internet for the price of a two course meal

61947826
>>61946600
Are you stupid? Why would this be bad? Everyone was bearish about this GOING AHEAD.

item.title: PREPARE FOR A BIG MARKET CRASH SOON — 44 replies — 176.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
PREPARE FOR A BIG MARKET CRASH SOON

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-03-07 01:33 UTC

OP

Bye bye rate cuts

Early replies (first 3)

61938721
>>61938715
The rate cut is an order. The Fed will comply or ICE sends them to Guatemala.

61938723
the new guy will do what hes told

61938731
If a putin profile pic twitter account says so it must be true

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61938922
>>61938715
get ready for fat retarded faggots crying on local news about not being able to fill up their F550 MEGASHART XXL sextuple cab dually ($1400/month payment for 96 months)

61939193
>>61938904
As an atheist, your post makes me roll my eyes, and fills me with nothing but contempt.
...But if i put myself in my possible future shoes, i am filled with dread and sadness for you, since you are bound for destruction in the fires of hell, your death eternal, as you obviously marvel after the beast.

61939200
You vill buy the electric car

61939202
>>61939193
Based

61939209
This is bidens economy you fucking bigots

61939246
>>61938904
No it isn't. The catholic church is the church that since Vatican II teaches (and still holds as dogma) that muslims are worshipping the "same god" and recently approved a bastardization of symbol of God's promise to man through Noah by way of heretically having a LGBT rainbow procession directly into the Vatican church, which (the Vatican) also now has A MUSLIM PRAYER ROOM. Popes endlessly shill and minister into the church for the "interfaith" globohomo movement, are COMPLETELY judaized and LITERALLY have the ROTHSCHILDS as the Vatican's bankers. Pope Francis participated in literally satanic religious rites publicly and said it was "for outreach," which is incoherent because early church dogma said that doing so was unforgiveable and grounds for ex-communication. Francis also said that evangelizing non-believers is "unchristian" and that the way we should spread the gospel is by being an example in communities and that Ramadan is "very important to many faiths, especially Christians." You are worshipping a synagogue of satan institution and need to find Christ who is indeed the way, the truth, and the life. You need to choose, conformity to the world and catholicism and your cultish culture identification with it, or Christ. They are mutually exclusive.

61939248
>>61938822
see
>>61939246

61939253
>>61938904
The original Catholic church maybe
Its been usurped for centuries now, anon
Tell me why there is a statue of lucifer behind the popes throne?
Why is the vaticans mass hall the mouth of a serpent?
These symbols are meaningful and hidden in plain sight

61939269
Ze future is Indians on electric bicycles commuting to work at zer pod

61939567
So cooked

61939587
>>61938715
Is it just cohencidence this coincides with CA closing refineries?

61940104
>>61938715
Wars aren't free, they'll do rate cuts and yield curve control to pay for everything. Stagflation will eat away at asset prices and savings accounts.

61940112
>>61938715
IM SCARED!

61940114
>mfw I drive a Prius

61940135
>>61938715
I voted for this

61940172
>>61938751

61940175
NIGGER JOCKEY JUST FLEW OVER MY HOUSE

61940828
>>61938715
>>61938721
THE ORACLE HAS SPOKEN.

61940861
>>61940828
Ok I'm going to cash.

61943680
>>61940828
Buffet timed the top with his retirement and gigantic cashpile.

61943714
>>61938715
well, obviously, I just decided to finally put the 100k I had lying around at only 3% into the market.
so naturally it must go to shit now, and will probably take 1-2 years to recover.

61943731
Can we just get it over with and let this shit all crash 80% already.
Sick of hearing about an "AI bubble" now everyone has forgotten that and hopped on the war train.
Whatever the cause, let's get this 2008 pt2 rekt bugaloo underway.

61944331
>>61938715
High interest rates is the only thing keeping this economy from hyperinflating and going the way of Weimar Germany. Unironically, a financial market correction would deflate stocks and commodity prices bringing purchasing power back into the dollar. Cutting interest rate, increasing lending and creating more fiat money will cause hyperinflation and make consumer food prices so expensive you will end up owning nothing and not being happy. Tight money=strong dollar, low rates combined with easy money equals weak dollar=more price inflation. The Fed needs to keep rates fixed and never print another new dollar this century.

61944336
>>61938762
Everywhere with a Central Bank is Jewish Owned.

61946551
Reported for being fake and gay.

61947609
>>61938751
tell me more about russian credibility

61947615
>>61944336
Yes. So all you gotta do is bet the opposite.
There is some in risk in that.

61947764
>>61943731
two more weeks

61947794
>>61939193
as an atheist you don't get to roll your eyes at anything but yourself lmao

61947800
>>61938752
Chinese ships will just get to pass and the rest won't.

item.title: THE BIGGEST CRASH SINCE COVID IS ABOUT TO HAPPEN — 43 replies — 172.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE BIGGEST CRASH SINCE COVID IS ABOUT TO HAPPEN

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-07 01:34 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61947028
>>61947021
Trump's done. He's getting raped in the courts now too.

61947037
>>61947021
Yea the biggest oil crash since covid is about to happen.

61947059
All of this was planned so kikes can buy up everything left on the cheap. The "if you didn't make it before 2025" guy was shitposting, but if you didn't buy bitcoin in 2011 and just hold without being a drug dealer or whatever you're boned forever and it will never get better. Welcome to the future.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61947138
>>61947062
you're retarded

61947145
>>61947021
wow, thats a lot of gains, time to get into oil

61947157
>>61947134
Seeethijg little kike. Look up who the number 1 buyer and owner of Iraq oil is in 2026

61947167
>>61947157
>Shits on self important yids
>must be a yid
Do they require you to be braindead to be a propagandist?

61947169
>>61947134
>the end of the petrodollar is not a real consequence
you know what, go right ahead, you people deserved this

61947183
>>61947169
>controls over 80% of global oil reserves
>end of the petrodollar
you do realize since the vuvuzela special law enforcement operation opec+ is dead when it comes to oil supply manipulations, Russia is a footnote and china can be degraded into regime change and cannibalism with the stroke of a pen. And for europe, I doubt that the current impotent batch of central planners is able to convince enough people like Hitler did to go die in the Caucasus or North Africa for ehhh, our democracy

61947185
So does this mean I'm not cooked for holding 10 shares of a 3x leveraged korean etf over the weekend? I feel like I'm about to be raped hard but we'll see.
>pic is me

61947203
>>61947077
Since Iran can also veto Gulf exports, does that mean all Gulf production is "under Iranian control"?

61947219
What happened with all the shale oil? Can't just unplug the wells when it hits $120.

61947289
>>61947203
There is no veto in international socialist organization when you risk a nuke by uncle sam

61947304
>>61947021
Israels MSCI is booming, 6% this week. I suppose the Jews are really gods chosen if their stock market can pump whilst fighting 2 wars and a genocide.

61947307
>>61947167
Modern leftists are just rabid dogs that attack with whatever ammo they think they have available. They are not sending their best

61947348
>>61947089
This, they are just as cucked as Russians, even though they are all about muh guns. Yeah muh guns they're not using.

61947390
>>61947348
Putin is at least a dictator, so Russians have some excuse.
Trump technically isn't a dictator. All the famous American "levers of democracy" are still in place and he can be legally stopped or removed from power. But Americans just don't care. They really don't care that their president does whatever he pleases and that he pisses in their face every day.

61947513
>>61947021
The biggest pump, you mean. This is a repeat of 2001 with potential to be 2001 + 1979.
Remember the dot com bubble?
Now we have the AI bubble.
Remember the war with Iraq?
Now we have the war with Iran.

2020 was the start of a new super cycle. The end of that super cycle is the popping of the AI bubble which will crash S&P and NVDA.

61947571
I thought the US would collapse slowly but this orange retard is going for a speedrun

61947582
>>61947307
That's makes them honestly as dangerous as the mask off left variation of Germanies 1930s variation. Contrary to their post-1945 variations that to some degree understood that they are just grifters and scammers that for the most part just performed the dance of the true believer, the current batch has been grown on their own propaganda as a dogmatic truth and nourished into blind belief by grifters and opportunists. I don't think I am taking it too far when calling for a preemptive culling in the interest of the right that after nearly 80 years of Boomer and Silent retardations finds back to its roots and unironically yids that despite having been not been innocent in being part of the nourishing group are still their favorite scapegoat

61947584
Im hiding in US Treasuries

61947585
>>61947118
>migger
Back to your containment board >>>/pol/

61947604
I should have bought hundreds of oil drums /biz/...

61947605
>>61947134
Lol

61947606
Doesn't the big cme gap this pump left behind mean it's gonna come back down at some point

61947611
>oil rapidly becoming irrelevant thanks to electric cars/trucks

But instead we'll stagnate ourselves to maintain status quo

61947639
>>61947611
What do you think creates the electricity you use, doofus ass?

61947665
>>61947582
I don't disagree
They've been fed a steady diet of reality distortion for so long they've been broken. Which also sounds harsh, but coming from academia, I was on the same path for a long time. The deprogramming takes a long time and leaves scars on the psyche that never really go away, especially after knowing what the big picture worldview is by these groups

61947674
>>61947390
>implying this is somehow different than every potus of the last 150 years, minus the GOAT Coolidge

61947713
>>61947665
I really don't want to know how those temples of the true believing priests have evolved since I jumped ship in the mid 10s. Still some part of me believes that for deprogramming shattering the core orthodoxy of the national socialists not being right wing but an offshoot and logical end point off the lefts own totalitarian, eugenic and utopian instinct might be a non bloody way to get some to vomit in disgust at their own mirror image and renounce. Though simply taking the rabies dogs behind the shed is the more save and economic rational lane

61947717
>>61947021
I voted for this

61947726
>>61947021
I didn't vote for this but I wish I did

61947804
Is there any case for a bullish market anytime in the next few months? Everything I’m seeing indicates it will be bear for the foreseeable future.

item.title: BAT bros I don't feel so good — 27 replies — 108.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BAT bros I don't feel so good

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-03-07 01:32 UTC

OP

Eich scammed us I just hope we get back to 20c so I can dump this shit

Early replies (first 3)

61942645
Nigga you have to wait a full fucking year. It dumps consistently in December-January, so buy now and dump then.

The eich nigger will never ever employ actual use cases for this token because he and his team are scared little faggots.

The president and his entire family have publicly rug pulled multiple times and nothing has come of it negatively for them. Brendan eich is just a scared little faggot do nothing bitch boy.

61942696
>>61942058
>>61942645
I solded after I saw Eich namedropped in the epstein files.

61942728
>>61942058
Who would've thought 27 cents would be the local top for BAT

the highs get lower

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61942922
BAT holders should all sue Eich for not doing more to enrich holders of his token which are essentially shares of his company

61943039
>>61942696
His inaction to make something he owns useful is bizarre. Anything would help ease the hatred for this token.

61943045
>>61943039
>something he sold
Ftfy

61943085
>>61943039
No one gives a shit about some whiny baggies on a basketweaving forum, least of all Eich. What, you're owed a fortune because some tech guy printed himself free money the same way every tech guy was doing? Fuck you, I hope Eich dumps it to zero, replaces BAT with stablecoins, and they get every use case in Brave you bitched about having.

61943088
>>61942645
>The president and his entire family have publicly rug pulled multiple times and nothing has come of it negatively for them. Brendan eich is just a scared little faggot do nothing bitch boy

How about Eich has more integrity than the scumbag nigger president and his scumbag nigger family. BAT will never be shit because the tokenomics are fucked beyond belief. None of the erc-20 tokens from previous cycles are gonna pump if alts finally get a chance unless they change their tokenomics to compete with the HYPE buy and burn model and if I remember correctly Brendan hates token burns

61943276
Sold my BAT at a huge loss years ago. I just lurk for thots

61943410
Are any of the OGs still around/still holding? Haven’t seen a BAT thread in a good while.

61944901
>>61943410
I'm an OG, have been holding since 2019 at least. Not gonna lie, it's been the most disappointing thing I've ever done in my life, but sunk cost fallacy is real and I'd rather just let it go to 0 than sell at this point

>>61943276
I don't blame you at all. I should've sold years ago too

61946835
does no one know what they are working on? i guess if you know u know

61946916
>>61943410
Still here and still holding a good amount of BAT, but I've dumped a lot.

>>61944901
>but sunk cost fallacy is real
It really is ;_;

>>61946835
Only thing I've heard lately is Brave Origin, but I'm not really around or looking into Brave/BAT like I used to.

61947061
>>61942696
Oh shit, was he really?

>>61943276
Sorry about your losses, lad. Hope you recouped.

61947085
>>61946916
you know what else you can dump? these webms

61947107
>>61943410
Since May 2017

61947151
>>61947061
who the fuck has washing machine in the kitchen. What tasteless whore. Let me guess, toilet bowl is in the bedroom?

61947166
>>61947085
Make a request, lad. I'll check every once in a while between my vidya grinding.

>>61947107
Based OG.

>>61947151
Based reposter. I dropped that one a few threads ago.

61947258
>>61946916
>>61947166
HOLY FUCKKKKK
got any big nigress booba

61947261
As shitty as bat is the community has always been solid

61947270
>>61944901
>>61946916
You're standing in front of a burning pile of money that you refuse to extinguish on the off chance that burning your money somehow makes you richer?

61947278
>>61947166
>>61947151
>>61947061
>>61946916
based thot poster

61947296
>>61943085
>hope Eich dumps it to zero, replaces BAT with stablecoins, and they get every use case in Brave you bitched about having.
The rational way to do this is Brendan launches his own stablefuck and he pays dividends with BAT. Problem is, he’s too smart to get in over his head with crapto. He was mentioned in the Epstein files but they couldn’t get him on the island. We’re not dealing with a retard like Brock Pierce or a based patriot like Nikolai from MKR. Brendan survives because he’s not too greedy or too political other his absolute hatred of faggots

61947372
>>61947296
Spoken like a terminal baggie. That's not rational at all. Make his own stablecoin so he can get raped by American finance? Then pay dividends in BAT so he can get raped again? Dividends for what? Paid to who? Why would a browser company get a banking license and why would they pay dividends instead of charge a fee so they can make a profit for once.

61947466
>>61947166
some gifted TnA in a razor thin bikini

61947742
>>61947258
I got you next post. Light or dark skinned?

>>61947270
I've been selling off a lot of BAT recently. Not at these prices though. I'll probably keep a good amount just because I'm that retarded.

I have other investments, I get a solid salary, and I have several income streams. Does it suck to lose out holding BAT? Sure, but it is what it is.

>>61947466
Like this? If not or you want a different body type, be as specific as you want.

61947792
>>61946916
its something else entirely. its big.

item.title: Put that memecoin down and buy defense stock NOW — 11 replies — 13.8/hr

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-07 00:53 UTC

OP

Put that memecoin down and buy defense stock NOW

Early replies (first 3)

61947000
>>61946983
Neocon ahhh administration. None of his cultists will ever draw the connection to this and what Cheney did.

61947011
>>61946983
>you better buy oil and defense stocks now Brah!!!

Already priced in. Late niggerbulls buying now will get rugged. Donnie Israel will not let oil be over 90$ a barrel going into spring driving season.

61947081
>>61947011
Dunno how little Donnie is going to stop that one.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61947098
>>61947081
>61947081
Donnie hates being unpopular. When Donnie Israels base starts to turn on him in the coming days, he will be forced to pull out. Donnie Israel is a showman. He does favors for favors, but his reputation means everything to him.

61947193

item.title: >biggest oil shock since 1973 >literally the worst case economic scenario >he STILL hasn't sold Some people just want to… — 60 replies — 29.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>biggest oil shock since 1973 >literally the worst case economic scenario >he STILL hasn't sold Some people just want to…

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-03-06 23:39 UTC

OP

>biggest oil shock since 1973
>literally the worst case economic scenario
>he STILL hasn't sold
Some people just want to lose money.

Early replies (first 3)

61942069
Iran will open the strait by the end of the week retard
they don't want to anger china and Europe

61942088
last time I checked bitcoin is mostly mined with clean coal and waterfall generators so tell me why it matters

61942092
May panic sell tomorrow. Already got fucked holding over one weekend

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61942752
>>61942120
>Why would they?
They did for some countries. Why? So they can sell China their oil and receive whatever they need from them for their war efforts.

61942774
>>61942570
touche anon touche

61942791
I trust my president, he'll find a way :^)

61942827
>>61942281

15-20m barrel deficit is what the market is looking at. Oil went to 120 because it worried about Russia’s 3m barrels getting sanctioned at the onset of Ukraine


THE ISSUE is that the daily 15-20m barrels is EATING the current surplus in the market that makes crude fair valued at $60. Once nations use their emergency reserves and storage, crude will enter a STRUCTURAL bull market. Even if everything goes back to normal, the extra 500m barrels in storage surplus are going to significantly raise the risk premium of crude.

Long story short, this is the foundation for STRUCTURAL inflation, not cyclical. We will have 2022 redux as bond yields force tightening globally.

61942865
how do i price a decade of high inflation in?

61942869
>>61942069
you can see the future? how come you're still poor?

61943169
>>61942065
80 is cheap 200 would be a shock

61943223
>>61942065
>literally the worst case economic scenario
You mean AI bubble?

61943248
The economy will collapse in two more weeks

61943302
>>61942065
>Trump can't back off because it would be a catastrophic look for the US if they lost to Iran in a fucking week
>also kikes would never allow it because of Kiketropolis "security concerns"
Anon....what are you waiting for....? Do you WANT to lose money?

61943311
>>61942069
FPBP literally that simple

61943436
>what is covid
Oil went bonkers like a dozen times since 2020, we had literally negative crud but muh 1973

61943514
>>61942065
It is moving quicker than I thought. I sold everything yesterday. Even my oil stocks, might miss out on the upside, but I have a suspicion that if there is a crash it is going to affect everything.

61943602
>>61942069
Trust the plan
Patriots are in control!

61943631
>>61943436
>what was covid
A perfect time to buy? So by that analogy now should be a perfect time to sell because oil is going in the opposite direction

61943663
I want to short. I really want. But this stupid ass market is completely delusional and gulping Trump's tweets like good sperm.
We had yet another illustration of this just two days ago when muh insurance mechanism, strait is open, navy will proteec ur ass, trust us, and thus oil started to dump.
Which obviously was a load of fucking bullshit as tankers got hit in the meantime.
And now the narrative being pushed is Shitran's ballistic missiles launch is going down exponentially. Well, sure, but they sure as fuck didn't hit tankers with fucking ballistic missiles.
One drone. One. One a day hitting a tanker.
That's all it takes to make the strait permanently closed.

But the fucktarded market being the fucktarded ape it is, it can't see that. no, it will trust any lie from the administration to pump it.
How is anyone supposed to short in these conditions, and how much can you fuck up with price discovery before it becomes a complete catastrophe spiling out of control.
I fucking hate these retards. Everyone in this dumb market, everyone in this fucktarded administration, and basically, every fucking one.

61943678
>>61942281
>exports mostly to china
>china needs oil
>china buys oil from other suppliers
>china slurps (You)r oil btw
>it's allowed because the west needs their temu shit
>oil price skyrockets globally

61943692
>>61943663
KWAB

61946145
>>61942558
>Reducing the supply of oil increases the price, since the demand will remain the same or increase.
No, the demand will not stay the same if the ones who demand it require it to keep demanding it in the first place. If the oil-importing economies can not sustain the expense of importation their economies will be less productive and have less money to import oil. If you had a population and during a drought you'd let some of them starve the demand for food during and after the drought would not stay the same as some people died and the other ones won't just start eating more simply because they survived.

>>61942752
>They did for some countries.
Most I heard was two tankers. I wouldn't call that a reopening of the strait.

61946502
>>61942069
I heard 2 more weeks from my sources

61946529
Last year around this time everybody was screaming about tarrifs ending the economy and wow would you look at that NOTHING HAPPENED

61946548
>>61942065
>biggest oil shock since 1973

61946711
>>61942069

lol

61946723
>>61942065

61947257
>>61942178
Shit nigga, 6 years? But /this time could be different/ if iran is ripped and the straight is opened

61947267
I won't sell because I have nothing to sell. I am dog shit stupid and I don't know anything about stocks or jewels or crypto I just come here for funsies

61947378
>>61942069
Its already open for china you fucking idiot

61947387
>>61942281

61947442
>>61942069
Modern Europe is a neutered whimpering dog. "Angering" them is a null point.

61947483
>>61943678
>Implying China will be getting even one (1) drop of oil from the world market after they're blockaded (Which is next after Iran)

Anon, the world will bend down and kiss America's boot and like it. Nobody will be able to force ships through the trade lanes the moment we just start sinking anything with a foreign flag that has not been cleared by the DoD.

item.title: The US has 10 days — 96 replies — 12.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-03-06 18:15 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61940113
>>61939905

Oh no!

61940124
>>61938341

>>61940124

61940234

61940472

61940500

61941344

61941358

We'll explode the nuklear just like in the bibble.

61941381
>>61941358

61941414
>>61938341

61941712
>>61941381

Ftfy

61942683
>>61938601
Ships have been transiting the strait by the simple expedient of turning off their transponders.

61942707
I don't mind if Israel and the US want to be retarded in their own little sandboxes, but why do they have to drag the rest of the world into it

61942764
>>61938506
The only thing that matters is that the US is the first to achieve AGI. >>61938487
Is on to something here. This move, which seems to be such a blunder, is the only thing that can set China and the EU back enough for the US to win the AI race.

61942831
>>61938341
>The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz
No.
>or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)
Nobody cares.
> on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day.
Ok, sot there's 1/4 of all oil that isn't getting to EU and UK.
So, oil price will go up.
>Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW
It'll be $100 - 150 .
I guess EU and UK should have not sanctioned Russia, tough luck now.

61942837
>>61938345
Wholly retard.
Trump made US oil independent during first term, and US is top 3 oil producing country now.
Oil price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget.

61942844
>>61942831

BOND YIELDS will crash the economy, not oil itself. With a structural 15m daily barrel deficit oil will go up every week. The only way to destroy demand is recession, oil will go up until it reaches the breaking point for global growth and blows everything up.

There is no feelings or opinions to this. Shutting the strait is a 15m barrel deficit. Ask any energy expert that that does to prices. It’s 150-200

61942850
>>61942837

Has nothing to do with US energy production because crude is a global market. Just ask yourself what would happen if the 30 year yield went to 7%. That’s a 30-45% dump in equities

61942851
>>61942837
I'm sure Joe Shcmo on the street is going to be happy paying 2 dollars a litre to fund Israel. Do you NEET retards really live in such a vacuum?

61942854
Crypto will pump. The thesis for crypto has always been based on the collapse of the system

61942897
>>61942854
Crypto is fiat with extra steps
No fiat, no crypto

61944577

If this continue another week we will see 125 WTI and markets down 5%

61944640
>>61938341

All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.
So the XRP domino theory is playing out?

61945025

61945048
>>61942837

61945281

item.title: Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted. — 73 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted.

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-03-06 17:56 UTC

OP

Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted. At least with scams like ICP, the baggies quickly knew they were scammed, the token went to zero and they could move on with their life and try to recover.

Instead Chainlink keeps the scam going for years and years, with new "partnerships" and "news" and "integrations", all while the token does nothing. They give just enough breadcrumbs to keep the baggies holding on desperately for a pump, and they probably keep buying even more LINK tokens as well ensuring they remain poor forever as they wait for a "mega moon" that never comes.

In that way it's actually a more brutal and evil scam, but still funny. It's crazy because all they had to do was buy Bitcoin... or gold, or the S&P 500, or literally anything else.

Early replies (first 3)

61931782
fuck all stinkies :}
hate em all hate em all
think they SUUUUCK xD

61931907
>>61931684
>At least with scams like ICP, the baggies quickly knew they were scammed, the token went to zero and they could move on with their life and try to recover.
Wait a minute. You're telling me I've DCA'd into a scam all these years?

61931931
thanks for keeping this a Chainlink board

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61938913
>>61934590
>He thinks it would happen irl

61938917
>>61932298
Cheap linkies again? Sign me up

61938935
Chainlink is unique among shitcoins though because it's more like a cult. I can remember a few years these people spamming up every forum I frequented including places that had nothing to do with crypto or finance. A deranged cackle of midwits who became giddy at every "partnership" and "on-chain utility" thinking they were so ahead of the market and that they were geniuses. That's why they are baggies, they are too emotionally invested and their ego cannot handle accepting that they were scammed. Most people who get rugged in a shitcoin scam at least know on some level that it's a pump and dump coin but that they were just too late to enter and too late to sell.

61938954
Cardano is worse
>tfw dropped $5000 on it back when it was $3 in 2021 like a retard
>it's currently $0.40
>been holding it patiently waiting to break even to get my money back

61939099
>>61932038
you do realize that if chainlink goes to zero people will be posting about it here for years, right?

61939107
>>61932327
>>61932513
>>61932516
>that link poster who is fully all-in dca no matter what also has millions available to buy a dip
personally I love stinks

61939365
>>61936147

bro let me spell it out for you: you. just. aren't. intimidating

>duuuuuuuude i sent someone a message on facebook 3 years ago
hahahhahaha

to put it another way, you got so mad about your online cult being disrespected, you went searching online for a random dude in the netherlands, and now you spend your days thinking about him jerking off

bahahahaha

i fucking BODIED you these last few years, and it's been very hard for you to come to grips with it

but you'll eventually take it in stride, because that's what you do

that's who you are. and who is that you ask....

THE Cuckolds of crypto

61939372
>>61938776
>This is good fud

in other words

>h h hehh guys, i'm t t TOTALLY cool with this
>a all part of the p p plan

bahahhaha you aren't fooling anyone kid


you don't have to pretend here, it's anonymous
just be yourself buddy

61939406
Artemis please come back I need my hopium.

61940963
>>61939365
Yawn
No one is convinced. You can keep barking, subhuman - but it's just the barking of a bbc simp cuck who won't have any resources to hide with when the time comes. You're the property of biz which is why you're doxxed, and why you're stuck here every day trying to get people to sell because it's the only thing you can think of to do to try to increase your odds later. That's how fuckin dumb you are lmfao.

61941143
>>61940963
>Yawn

you've been yawning for years now. take a nap already kid

>No one is convinced
who exactly are you speaking for here. the chart looks pretty convinced (you're down 80)

>but it's just the barking of a bbc simp cuck who won't have any resources to hide with when the time comes.

bahahhahahhaahaha

dude you just aren't hard. how many times do i have to tell ya


>lmfao

it's not authentic to say that when you're in tears

61941730
>>61941143
Browns don't understand why white people know they are brown just by their typing. It's honestly incredibly easy to spot you. It's lame at this point. I just hope you hold Link and this is just a way to vent your frustration away. Truly sad to see

61941797
>>61932671
Yeah bro I was banking on it hitting $90 at least last year and now my life is fucked.
I'm surprising emotionally alright though but if I sat down and took the time to comprehend the opportunity cost I would want to rope immediately.

>I'm better off focusing on a high income career than waiting for this shit
This is how I'm coping too.

61942302
>>61931684
>scam
no actually
they make money and buy back the token
they have been doing 1.1 million USD buybacks every week: https://x.com/chainlink/status/2029618640916447458

61942449
5 reasons LINK will fail

1) Token necessity is unclear
Customers want reliable data/messaging, not volatility. If adoption grows, it can be structured as a service (fiat/stables, contracts, OTC), making LINK optional rather than required.

2) Partnerships ≠ production
Many “partnerships” are pilots, programs, or compatibility announcements with limited measurable throughput. The market can end up pricing logo density instead of fee density.

3) Value accrual may bypass holders
Even if usage exists, economics can flow mainly to node operators and negotiated counterparties, while token holders get narrative upside without proportional cash-flow linkage.

4) Staking doesn’t prove demand
Yield matters only if funded by sustainable fees. If rewards are modest or subsidy-dependent, staking is retention/marketing, not an economic flywheel.

5) Commoditization risk
Oracles/messaging can be competed by alternatives, specialized providers, rollup-native solutions, or “good enough” centralized feeds. If switching is feasible, pricing power compresses.

What would falsify this bear case: multi-quarter, auditable growth in fee revenue tied to on-chain usage; clear mechanics forcing LINK acquisition/holding; scaled CCIP/oracle volumes with transparent unit economics; staking materially funded by fees.

61943363
linkies deserve the following: pain, misery, poverty, and humiliation :} (but mostly poverty, tbqh)

61943390
>>61942449
Actually based AI

61943409
what a weird ICP thread

61943560
>>61940963
Bro you got owned take the L and move on Adem won simple as

61943789
>>61940963

cuckolds LOST

61943818
What does link even do? Seems like it only sells links. No different than cardano

61943829
>>61943818
it's basically a cuckold club, Linkies brag about all the money they lost over the past 5 years and call you a fuddie/peeler/Bulgarian if you make fun of their disastrous investment.

61944794
>>61941730

bro half the chainlink team is minorities. this just doesn't hit like it used to

not to mention your cult leader being a slavic scam artist lmfao

so sit the fuck down


>>61943818
>What does link even do? Seems like it only sells links

it's a fundraising coin for chainlink labs ltd, which is the operating company based in the cayman islands. your donations allow the company to operate, and pays the salaries of 700 people and their diversity initiatives, women in the blockchain movements, and teaching poor indians and africans how to use computers at hackathons

61944811
>>61943818
>No different than cardano

spot on. cardano is the reddit version of link, and vice versa

61944828

61944871

61944890
>>61944828
Is this bullish or bearish? I can't tell anymore.

61944919
>>61931684
The idea that the world will ever run on the backbone of Ethereum is hilarious. The entire thing is Sci-Fi vaporware.

61945720
ooohhhh stupid cuuuuckolds
you can't stop huuuumiliating
youuuurseeelveees
buying Link tokeeeeens
that keep craaaashing

61945747
>>61931684
Jannies lost all their linkies so now they spend all day fudding link. You can't make this up. KEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEK
